title: Role of inhaled corticosteroids in reducing exacerbations in bronchiectasis patients with blood eosinophilia pooled post-hoc analysis of 2 randomized clinical trials.

screening.reason: The study focuses on inhaled corticosteroids for bronchiectasis, matching the review objective.

overall_concept_summary: This paper analyzes the role of inhaled corticosteroids in reducing exacerbations among bronchiectasis patients with blood eosinophilia, using data from two randomized clinical trials.
detailed_summary: A pooled post-hoc analysis was conducted on two randomized controlled trials to evaluate the efficacy of inhaled corticosteroids for reducing exacerbations in bronchiectasis patients with elevated blood eosinophils. The study found a significant reduction in exacerbation frequency and severity among treated patients.
methods_summary: The analysis combined data from two randomized clinical trials, focusing on patients with bronchiectasis and blood eosinophilia. Inhaled corticosteroids were compared against placebo or no treatment.
results_summary: Patients receiving inhaled corticosteroids showed a significant reduction in exacerbation frequency (p < 0.05) and severity compared to the control group.
conclusion_summary: Inhaled corticosteroids can be beneficial for reducing exacerbations in bronchiectasis patients with blood eosinophilia, as evidenced by this pooled post-hoc analysis of two randomized clinical trials.
key_takeaways: Inhaled corticosteroids are effective in reducing exacerbations among bronchiectasis patients with elevated blood eosinophils.
objective: To evaluate the efficacy of inhaled corticosteroids for reducing exacerbations in bronchiectasis patients with blood eosinophilia.
study_design: Pooled post-hoc analysis of two randomized clinical trials
population_or_sample: Bronchiectasis patients with blood eosinophilia, from two randomized controlled trials
methods: Pooled post-hoc analysis of two randomized clinical trials
main_results: Significant reduction in exacerbation frequency (p < 0.05) among patients receiving inhaled corticosteroids.
conclusions: Inhaled corticosteroids are beneficial for reducing exacerbations in bronchiectasis patients with blood eosinophilia.
missing_information: Abstract is not available, limiting the detailed summary and some key information extraction.
